nodes.label: SPM_BG / *_BG ambient-field global API
nodes.rationale: All dashboards expose a tiny global background API (setAccent/setGlass/setCondition) on window so app logic can drive the WebGL field without a hard dependency; the API is defined up-front as no-ops so calls are safe before init or when WebGL is unavailable. COCOM/ECG/formulary use window.SPM_BG (setAccent + setGlass); humanoid reuses the PFC field under window.PFC_BG (setCondition + setGlass).

nodes.label: Standalone single-file inline build pattern
nodes.rationale: Each dashboard ships an offline *_Standalone.html built by build_standalone.py, which reads index.html and string-replaces each <link>/<script src> tag with inline <style>/<script> blocks, escaping </script as <\/script. Humanoid extends the pattern: it also base64-inlines fonts and Spline scene/WASM/JS as data: URLs.

nodes.label: EDITMODE tweak host postMessage protocol
nodes.rationale: useTweaks/TweaksPanel implement a host edit-mode protocol over postMessage: panel posts __edit_mode_available, listens for __activate/__deactivate_edit_mode, and persists edits via __edit_mode_set_keys (host rewrites the /*EDITMODE-BEGIN*/ JSON block on disk). TWEAK_DEFAULTS is the single source of truth consumed by scene-app's App.

nodes.label: Cross-component sharing via window.* registry
nodes.rationale: Components communicate by reading/writing window.* globals (PCC_SCENARIOS, PCC_SOUND, PFC_BG, ECG_LEADS, registered components). This lets the multi-file build and the single-file standalone build (each component IIFE-wrapped) share state identically without a module bundler.

nodes.label: EDITMODE host/postMessage tweak protocol
nodes.rationale: Tweak values live in a /*EDITMODE-BEGIN*/ JSON block; useTweaks posts __edit_mode_set_keys to the host which rewrites the block on disk. App and TweaksPanel sync open/close state through __activate/__deactivate/__dismissed postMessages.

nodes.label: Patient-condition-driven ambient field
nodes.rationale: The WebGL background colour tracks scenario priority (stable/urgent/critical) and heartbeat tempo follows HR, giving an at-a-glance ambient read of patient state; pharm reuses the same engine but drives it by drug tier and prompts by active category.

nodes.label: Scenario Card Structure (OPQRST/Vitals/Dx/Q&A/Actions/Pearls)
nodes.captured_at: Each scenario follows a fixed didactic template: Character Development, Environment, Clinical Presentation, OPQRST, Vital Signs, Physical Examination, hidden Differential Diagnosis, answer-keyed Critical-Thinking Questions, Critical Actions checklist, Clinical Pearls, and Resolution. Reveal/toggle interactions support Socratic self-testing.

nodes.norm_label: rabies pep (wound wash + rig + vaccine series)

nodes.label: VHF Infection-Control / Isolation Posture
nodes.captured_at: Undifferentiated hemorrhagic fevers are treated as potentially transmissible (Ebola/Marburg cannot be excluded clinically) until PCR confirmation; default to standard+contact+droplet precautions while not delaying life-saving evacuation.

nodes.label: WHO Rabies Exposure Categories (I/II/III)
nodes.captured_at: Category III transdermal bite mandates immediate wound washing, full vaccine series, and RIG; rabies once symptomatic is essentially 100% fatal so PEP is the only lever.

nodes.label: Disease and Non-Battle Injury (DNBI) Dominance
nodes.captured_at: In the AFRICOM AOR the dominant medical threat is disease and non-battle injury, not gunfire — driving the tropical/infectious-disease emphasis of the scenario set.

nodes.label: AFRICOM (U.S. Africa Command)
nodes.rationale: All scenarios in this range are AFRICOM AOR cases (Central African rainforest, Ethiopian highlands, Horn of Africa, Somalia) using the 'af-sc' id prefix.

nodes.label: Ivermectin-Induced Encephalopathy (high Loa load)
nodes.rationale: Fatal encephalopathy when ivermectin is given to patients with very high Loa microfilarial loads (>~30,000 mf/mL).

nodes.label: Test-and-Not-Treat Strategy
nodes.rationale: Measure daytime-blood Loa microfilarial load before ivermectin; withhold if load is dangerously high. The core safeguard against iatrogenic encephalopathy.

nodes.label: High-Altitude Cerebral Edema (HACE)
nodes.rationale: AMS progressed to brain swelling; cardinal signs ataxia + altered mentation.

nodes.label: High-Altitude Pulmonary Edema (HAPE)
nodes.rationale: Non-cardiogenic pulmonary edema; leading cause of altitude death.

nodes.label: Descent (definitive treatment for HACE/HAPE)
nodes.rationale: Descent directly reverses the hypoxic driver; drugs and oxygen are bridges, not substitutes.

nodes.label: Wilderness Medical Society (WMS) Guidance
nodes.rationale: Cited for graded-ascent prophylaxis (altitude) and 3% hypertonic saline dosing (EAH).

nodes.label: Doxycycline
nodes.rationale: Recurs across cases: brucellosis combination tx, and empiric leptospirosis/rickettsial coverage.

nodes.label: Avoid Rifampin where TB Co-Endemic
nodes.rationale: Antimicrobial-stewardship choice: prefer doxycycline+aminoglycoside over doxycycline+rifampin to avoid promoting rifampin-resistant TB.

nodes.label: One-Health / Zoonotic Partner-Force Medicine
nodes.rationale: Human-animal-environment interface framing shared by brucellosis and leptospirosis cases.

nodes.label: Empiric Doxycycline Covers Rickettsial Mimic
nodes.rationale: In East Africa, doxycycline covers both leptospirosis and endemic rickettsial disease when undifferentiated; penicillin/cephalosporins do not.

nodes.label: Ceftriaxone
nodes.rationale: Used for severe leptospirosis and, per TCCC Change 25-1, as the open-combat-wound antibiotic replacing ertapenem.

nodes.label: Scenario 24 — Sickle Cell Trait & Exertional Rhabdomyolysis (Operation Iron Selection)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 24 — sickle cell trait & exertional rhabdomyolysis (operation iron selection)

nodes.label: Hyperkalemia Algorithm (calcium / insulin+dextrose / remove)
nodes.rationale: Acute killer in rhabdo and crush syndrome; same recurring austere-care algorithm referenced in Scenario 40.

nodes.label: Nelson et al., NEJM 2016 (Army SCT cohort)
nodes.author: Nelson et al.
nodes.rationale: ~48,000-soldier cohort: SCT not linked to higher death under precautions but linked to higher exertional rhabdomyolysis — the evidence-updated framing.
nodes.norm_label: nelson et al., nejm 2016 (army sct cohort)

nodes.label: Rectal Core Temperature (key discriminator)
nodes.rationale: Single most useful field discriminator separating exertional heat stroke (>40C) from rhabdo and EAH.

nodes.label: Exercise-Associated Hyponatremia (EAH)
nodes.rationale: Dilutional water intoxication; the collapse you must NOT treat with more water — treatment is opposite of dehydration.

nodes.label: Drink-to-Thirst Prevention
nodes.rationale: Counters the 'overdrink to beat dehydration' dogma that causes EAH; the conscientious heavy drinker is the classic victim.

nodes.label: Neurotoxic Elapid Envenomation (Black Mamba / Cobra)
nodes.rationale: Descending paralysis with minimal local injury; mirror image of cytotoxic viper bite.

nodes.label: Pressure Immobilization (indicated for neurotoxic bite)
nodes.rationale: Indicated for neurotoxic elapid bites without local necrosis — opposite of the contraindication for cytotoxic viper bites.

nodes.label: Assisted Ventilation (the lifesaver)
nodes.rationale: A paralyzed but ventilated patient survives; the ventilation marathon links envenomation to prolonged care.

nodes.label: Junctional Hemorrhage
nodes.rationale: Groin/axilla/neck bleed too proximal for a limb tourniquet — leading preventable death in the post-tourniquet era.

nodes.label: Whole Blood / Walking Blood Bank (LTOWB)
nodes.rationale: Low-titer O whole blood is TCCC/JTS resuscitation fluid of choice; ROLO / Valkyrie programs; recurs in Scenarios 29, 33, 36.

nodes.label: Tranexamic Acid (TXA, single 2 g dose)
nodes.rationale: Current TCCC simplified to single 2 g IV/IO; 3-hour window removed (Ukraine data, benefit to 12 h).

nodes.label: MARCH Algorithm (TCCC)
nodes.rationale: Massive hemorrhage first; structural sequence referenced across all combat-trauma scenarios (27-33).

nodes.label: TCCC Change 25-1 (ceftriaxone for open wounds)
nodes.rationale: Doctrinal update: ceftriaxone 2 g replaced ertapenem for open combat wounds; cited in Scenarios 27 and 41.

nodes.label: Scenario 28 — Tension Pneumothorax (Operation Hollow Reed)
nodes.rationale: Two-site needle decompression and finger thoracostomy; MARCH Respiration.

nodes.label: Scenario 29 — Hemorrhagic Shock / Damage-Control Resuscitation (Operation Crimson Ledger)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 29 — hemorrhagic shock / damage-control resuscitation (operation crimson ledger)

nodes.label: Scenario 30 — Airway Trauma & Surgical Cricothyroidotomy (Operation Shattered Jaw)
nodes.rationale: TCCC Change 24-1 airway positioning and surgical cric for maxillofacial trauma.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 30 — airway trauma & surgical cricothyroidotomy (operation shattered jaw)

nodes.label: Scenario 31 — Traumatic Brain Injury / Blast TBI (Operation Glass Ridge)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 31 — traumatic brain injury / blast tbi (operation glass ridge)

nodes.label: Scenario 32 — Burn Management (Operation Fuel Fire)
nodes.rationale: Rule of Tens, inhalation injury, fluid discipline.

nodes.label: Scenario 33 — Pelvic Fracture & Traumatic Amputation (Operation Broken Anvil)
nodes.rationale: Pelvic binder, tourniquet, and blood.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 33 — pelvic fracture & traumatic amputation (operation broken anvil)

nodes.label: Scenario 34 — TCCC Triple-Option Analgesia (Operation Steady Hand)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 34 — tccc triple-option analgesia (operation steady hand)

nodes.label: Ketamine (analgesia)
nodes.rationale: Recurs as analgesia for shocked/dressing-change patients across trauma and PCC scenarios.

nodes.label: Scenario 35 — TCCC to PCC Transition (Operation Long Hold)

nodes.norm_label: scenario 35 — tccc to pcc transition (operation long hold)

nodes.label: MARC²H³-PAWS-L Framework

nodes.label: Prolonged Casualty Care (PCC / PFC)
nodes.rationale: Organizing theme for Scenarios 35-41: sustaining care over days far from definitive surgery.

nodes.label: Scenario 36 — Prolonged Damage-Control Resuscitation (Operation Crimson Reserve)
nodes.rationale: Sustaining transfusion over days.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 36 — prolonged damage-control resuscitation (operation crimson reserve)

nodes.label: Scenario 37 — Nursing Fundamentals in PCC (Operation Quiet Vigil)
nodes.rationale: RAVINE / HITMAN nursing mnemonics — the boring care that saves lives.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 37 — nursing fundamentals in pcc (operation quiet vigil)

nodes.label: Scenario 38 — Telemedicine in Prolonged Field Care (Operation Distant Voice)
nodes.norm_label: scenario 38 — telemedicine in prolonged field care (operation distant voice)

nodes.label: Scenario 39 — Sepsis in Prolonged Field Care (Operation Slow Burn)
nodes.rationale: 'Hypotension is a late sign'; referenced by wound-sepsis surveillance in Scenarios 40 and 41.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 39 — sepsis in prolonged field care (operation slow burn)

nodes.label: Scenario 40 — Crush Syndrome & Hyperkalemia (Operation Fallen Wall)
nodes.rationale: Danger of releasing the casualty; shares the hyperkalemia algorithm with rhabdomyolysis.
nodes.norm_label: scenario 40 — crush syndrome & hyperkalemia (operation fallen wall)

nodes.rationale: Header at lines 2037-2042 (within range); leave war wounds open, copious irrigation, conservative debridement, delayed primary closure.
